perturbation.original_text: A. Guarantors shall pay or cause to be paid to Franchisor all monies payable by Master Franchisee under the Master Franchise Agreement on the days and times and in the manner therein appointed for payment thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: A. Guarantors shall pay or cause to be paid to Franchisor all undisputed monies payable by Master Franchisee under the Master Franchise Agreement within 90 days of the date specified in the invoice, provided that the invoice is valid and undisputed. Payment for disputed amounts will be suspended until resolution, at which point payment is due within 30 days.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding payment terms. Initially, guarantors had to pay according to the Master Franchise Agreement's terms. Now, payment is tied to 'undisputed' invoices and allows for a 90-day payment window, plus suspension for disputes, contradicting the original agreement's specified payment schedule which is reffered to in point B, which states that 'Guarantors shall unconditionally guarantee full performance and discharge by Master Franchisee of all the obligations of Master Franchisee under the Master Franchise Agreement at the times and in the manner therein provided.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. Guarantors shall unconditionally guarantee full performance and discharge by Master Franchisee of all the obligations of Master Franchisee under the Master Franchise Agreement at the times and in the manner therein provided.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces payment terms related to 'undisputed' invoices and a 90-day payment window, directly contradicting the contradicted text which states that the guarantors guarantee full performance of the Master Franchisee's obligations under the Master Franchise Agreement 'at the times and in the manner therein provided.' The original agreement specified payment schedules, which the modified text overrides, creating a clear conflict and inconsistency regarding when payment is due. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where conflicting deadlines or obligations are created.

perturbation.original_text: D. Franchisor shall not be obligated to proceed against Master Franchisee or exhaust any security from Master Franchisee or pursue or exhaust any remedy, including any legal or equitable relief against Master Franchisee, before proceeding to enforce the obligations of the Guarantors under this Guarantee, and the enforcement of such obligations may take place before, after, or contemporaneously with, enforcement of any debt or obligation of Master Franchisee under the Master Franchise Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: D. Franchisor shall be obligated to first proceed against Master Franchisee and exhaust all available security and remedies, including legal and equitable relief, against Master Franchisee before seeking recourse from the Guarantors under this Guarantee. Enforcement against the Guarantors can only commence after demonstrating a good faith effort to recover from the Master Franchisee has failed. Additionally, the enforcement against the Guarantors can only be pursued up to 50% of the outstanding debt of Master Franchisee.
perturbation.explanation: This creates a direct conflict with the original clause which stated the Franchisor was *not* obligated to pursue the Master Franchisee first. Now, the Franchisor *must* exhaust all options against the Master Franchisee before pursuing the Guarantors, directly contradicting the previous freedom. In Section E it states that, 'Without affecting the Guarantors' obligations under this Guarantee, Franchisor, without notice to the Guarantors, may extend, modify, or release any indebtedness or obligation of Master Franchisee, or settle, adjust, or compromise any claims against Master Franchisee.' This is another contradiction, because, Franchisor has an obiligation now to pursue Master Franchisee first.
perturbation.contradicted_text: E. Without affecting the Guarantors' obligations under this Guarantee, Franchisor, without notice to the Guarantors, may extend, modify, or release any indebtedness or obligation of Master Franchisee, or settle, adjust, or compromise any claims against Master Franchisee. Guarantors waive notice of amendment of the Master Franchise Agreement and notice of demand for payment or performance by Master Franchisee.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text allowed the Franchisor to pursue the Guarantors without first pursuing the Master Franchisee. The changed text now *requires* the Franchisor to first pursue the Master Franchisee, conflicting with the original allowance. Further, the changed text also goes against section E which states that the Franchisor can modify or release any obligation of Master Franchisee. This would mean it directly impacts the 'Guarantors obligations' to the contrary of Section E.

perturbation.original_text: F. Guarantors' obligations under this Guarantee shall remain in full force and effect, and shall be unaffected by: (1) the unenforceability of the Master Franchise Agreement against Master Franchisee; (2) the termination of any obligations of Master Franchisee under the Master Franchise Agreement by operation of law or otherwise; (3) the bankruptcy, insolvency, dissolution, or other liquidation of Master Franchisee, including, without limitation, any surrender or disclaimer of the Franchise Agreement by the trustee in bankruptcy of Master Franchisee; (4) Franchisor's consent or acquiescence to any bankruptcy, receivership, insolvency, or any other creditor's proceedings of or against Master Franchisee, or by the winding-up or dissolution of Master Franchisee, or any other event or occurrence which would have the effect at law of terminating the existence of Master Franchisee's obligations prior to the termination of the Master Franchise Agreement; or (5) by any other agreements or other dealings between Franchisor and Master Franchisee having the effect of amending or altering the Master Franchise Agreement or Master Franchisee's obligations under this Guarantee, or by any want of notice by Franchisor to Master Franchisee of any default of Master Franchisee or by any other matter, thing, act, or omission of Franchisor whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: F. Guarantors' obligations under this Guarantee shall be automatically terminated if: (1) the Master Franchise Agreement is deemed unenforceable against Master Franchisee for reasons of regulatory non-compliance; (2) Master Franchisee successfully renegotiates the Master Franchise Agreement with Franchisor, resulting in a material reduction of financial obligations; (3) Master Franchisee declares bankruptcy and the trustee in bankruptcy rejects the Franchise Agreement, leaving no prospect of recovery; (4) Franchisor agrees to a settlement with Master Franchisee in a bankruptcy proceeding that releases Master Franchisee from a substantial portion of its debt; or (5) Franchisor materially breaches its obligations under the Master Franchise Agreement, impacting Master Franchisee's ability to perform. For any other cases, Guarantors' obligations under this Guarantee shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a contradiction. Initially, the guarantor's obligations were explicitly *unaffected* by various scenarios like unenforceability or bankruptcy of the Master Franchisee. Now, the guarantor's obligations are *automatically terminated* under specific conditions related to these same scenarios. This introduces significant uncertainty and completely changes the nature of the guarantee. This contradicts section A, where 'Guarantors shall pay or cause to be paid to Franchisor all undisputed monies payable by Master Franchisee under the Master Franchise Agreement within 90 days of the date specified in the invoice, provided that the invoice is valid and undisputed. Payment for disputed amounts will be suspended until resolution, at which point payment is due within 30 days.', since it no longer guarantees the payments, under this added condition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. Guarantors shall pay or cause to be paid to Franchisor all undisputed monies payable by Master Franchisee under the Master Franchise Agreement within 90 days of the date specified in the invoice, provided that the invoice is valid and undisputed. Payment for disputed amounts will be suspended until resolution, at which point payment is due within 30 days.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces specific conditions under which the Guarantor's obligations are automatically terminated. This directly contradicts the original text, which stated that the Guarantor's obligations would be unaffected by events such as the Master Franchisee's bankruptcy. This also contradicts section A, which states the Guarantors *shall* pay all undisputed monies, now under the added conditions, the payments are no longer guaranteed.
